steps.title: Identify the thermal barrier to superfluorescence
steps.content: The passage explains that superfluorescence was previously only observed at extremely cold temperatures because “dipoles cannot synchronize at higher temperatures.” This identifies the challenge that needs to be solved.

steps.title: Apply the mechanism of polarons to the answer choices
steps.content: Polarons protect dipoles from thermal interference, enabling synchronization despite the higher temperature—choice B's statement exactly. The other choices describe unrelated effects not addressed in the passage's explanation. This confirms B is correct.
